text: BREAKING: Iran FM Araghchi declares Strait of Hormuz 'COMPLETELY OPEN' for all commercial vessel traffic during ceasefire period. This is the biggest de-escalation signal since the war began. Dow futures immediately +500pts. Oil reversing hard — Brent dropping from $99 toward $93. If this holds, it's a game-changer for global shipping and energy markets.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, CNBC, Washington Times, NBC News confirmed.

text: Pentagon briefing: Hegseth 'locked and loaded' on Iran energy infrastructure. Gen. Caine: 'literally a moment's notice' for combat ops. 13 ships turned back by blockade. BUT — Iran just opened Hormuz for commercial traffic. Two contradictory signals. Blockade of Iranian ports continues while strait opens. Markets confused but optimistic.
verificationNote: Fox News, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, CNBC confirmed both developments.

text: Western intel assessment: Iran retains roughly 50% of missile launchers intact + thousands of attack drones despite 5+ weeks of US/Israeli strikes. Tunnel networks prepared over decades protecting arsenal. Soufan Center confirms 'missile and drone arsenal remains potent.' This is significant — means Iran retains meaningful deterrent capability even after massive bombardment.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Soufan Center confirmed intel assessment.

text: Hezbollah fired 40+ rockets at northern Israel just hours before the ceasefire — 1 severely wounded in Carmiel. Then issued statement: fighters' 'fingers remain on the trigger.' Lebanon ceasefire is now 'mostly holding' per Al-Monitor but violations reported — Israeli shelling at Khiam/Dibbine 30 min after midnight. Macron: 'concerned ceasefire may already be undermined.'
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Middle East Eye, LBC, Al-Monitor confirmed.

text: USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) strike group rerouting via Cape of Good Hope to avoid Houthi threats in Bab el-Mandeb. Currently off Namibia/southern Africa with escorts USS Donald Cook, Mason, Ross. ~5,000 sailors. Will join USS Abraham Lincoln in Arabian Sea. Total US naval force in region approaching 3 carrier groups when Bush arrives late April.
verificationNote: USNI News, Stars and Stripes, Navy Times confirmed.

text: Day 49 BREAKING roundup: HORMUZ OPEN — Iran FM Araghchi declares 'completely open' for commercial vessels during ceasefire. OIL CRASHES — Brent $93.59 (+17.2%), WTI $89.90 (+19.1%), down from $99.39 earlier. Hegseth 'locked and loaded.' 40+ Hezbollah rockets pre-ceasefire (1 wounded Carmiel). Pezeshkian: '40-day resistance.' Pope Leo: 'world ravaged by tyrants.' Iran retains 50% missile capability. S&P record 7,023. Gas $4.076 (tick lower). Trump: deal 'very close.' Ceasefire expires April 22 — 5 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Bloomberg, CNBC, Fox News, Times of Israel, Press TV, NPR, Al Jazeera, PBS, AAA.

text: TRUMP BOMBSHELL: Posted on Truth Social — Israel 'PROHIBITED from bombing Lebanon by the U.S.A.' Netanyahu and team caught completely off guard. Axios: Israeli officials asked White House for clarification — post 'contradicted the agreement.' Netanyahu responded publicly: 'We haven't finished the job yet.' Haaretz: 'Trump hijacked Israeli foreign policy.' Unprecedented US language toward Israel.
verificationNote: Reuters, Axios, NYT, Haaretz confirmed Trump post and Netanyahu's response.

text: CEASEFIRE VIOLATION Day 1: Israeli drone strike kills 1 between Kounine-Beit Yahoun in south Lebanon — first fatality since truce. 3 wounded. IDF also attacked ambulance team in Kunin with machinegun + artillery. UNIFIL confirmed shelling until 6 AM + airspace violations. Meanwhile thousands of Lebanese civilians defied IDF warnings and forced their way past barriers south of Litani River.
verificationNote: CBC News, Al Jazeera, Reuters confirmed all violations.

text: OIL SETTLEMENT CRASH: Brent settles $90.38 (-9% on day, +13.3% from pre-war). WTI $83.85 (-11.5% on day). Biggest single-day drop since April 8 ceasefire. Markets celebrating Hormuz opening + deal optimism. S&P 500 record 7,126. Nasdaq 13-day winning streak — longest since 1992. Dow +869 pts. BUT: Maersk says needs 'further guarantees' before resuming Hormuz transit. Insurance still 5x normal. Zero oil tankers transiting.
verificationNote: Reuters, CNBC, TheStreet, Investopedia confirmed settlement prices and market records.

text: EUROPEAN HORMUZ MISSION: Macron-Starmer Paris summit concluded — ~30 countries plan UK-led defensive naval force for mine clearance and commercial shipping protection. Germany, Italy attending in person. Over a dozen offered naval assets. Trump furious — called NATO allies 'useless' and told them to 'STAY AWAY.' Explicit rejection of joining US blockade. Military planning meeting next week.
verificationNote: Reuters, Politico EU, AP News confirmed summit and Trump response.

text: DEAL FRAMEWORK: Axios exclusive — US considering $20B cash-for-uranium deal. Would release frozen Iranian funds for Iran's 2,000kg enriched uranium stockpile (including 450kg at 60% purity). Iran demanded $27B; US initially offered $6B. Three-page MoU for 60-day negotiation being finalized. Pakistan FM: 'very close to stitching a deal.' Next talks expected this weekend Islamabad. ISW: Iran exploiting ceasefire to regenerate missile forces at tunnel entrances.
verificationNote: Axios exclusive sourced to 3 senior Iranian officials. ISW confirmed Iran missile regeneration assessment.

text: Day 49 PM roundup: TRUMP PROHIBITS Israel bombing Lebanon. Israeli drone kills 1 post-ceasefire (first fatality). Brent settles $90.38 (-9%), WTI $83.85 (-11.5%) — biggest crash since April 8. S&P record 7,126. Nasdaq 13-day streak. European Hormuz mission (~30 countries). $20B uranium deal under discussion. Katz: Israel holds 10km security zone indefinitely. Guterres: Hormuz opening 'step in right direction.' Gas $4.093. Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Ceasefire expires April 22 — 5 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Reuters, Axios, CBC News, CNBC, Politico EU, UN News, AAA.

text: RAS ISSA STRIKE: US forces struck Ras Issa oil port in Hodeidah, Yemen — 74 killed, 171+ injured. Deadliest single US strike in Operation Rough Rider. First attack on Houthi-controlled oil facility. Massive fireballs destroyed tanker trucks and port infrastructure. Guterres 'gravely concerned.' Houthi media broadcasting graphic footage.
verificationNote: PBS News, New Arab, Al Mayadeen confirmed strike and casualty figures.

text: HOUSE WAR POWERS: Resolution fails 213-214 — ONE vote short. Only Rep. Massie (R-KY) crossed party lines. Senate blocked 47-52 on April 15. 60-day War Powers clock approaching April 29. Fifth time Congress has attempted to restrain Trump's Iran war authority. Bipartisan antiwar coalition growing but still insufficient.
verificationNote: NBC News, Democracy Now confirmed vote tally and party breakdown.

text: OIL REBOUND: Brent rebounds to $95.86 (+20.1% from pre-war), WTI $90.79 (+20.2%) after massive April 17 crash. Markets realizing US CENTCOM blockade of Iranian ports continues despite Hormuz opening. Iran state media walks back Araghchi — says he 'misspoke.' Gold surges to $4,867/oz (+41.7% YoY). Sanctions waiver expires tomorrow April 19. EIA raised 2026 Brent forecast to $96/bbl average.
verificationNote: Trading Economics, Investing.com, EIA confirmed oil rebound. Sherwood News confirmed Araghchi walkback.

text: PEZESHKIAN LETTER: Iran president publishes second open letter to American people — 'Iran harbours no enmity toward ordinary Americans.' Calls portrayal of Iran as threat 'inconsistent with history.' Separately: Iran Parliament Speaker Qalibaf warns 'If blockade continues, Hormuz will not remain open.' IRGC Navy warns any military vessel approaching strait = ceasefire violation.
verificationNote: Iran LiveUAMap, Tribune India confirmed letter. NBC News confirmed Qalibaf warning.

text: Day 50 PM roundup: RAS ISSA — US strikes Yemen oil port, 74 killed (deadliest single US strike). HOUSE — War powers fails 213-214 (one vote). OIL — Brent rebounds $95.86, WTI $90.79. Iran walks back Hormuz opening. PEZESHKIAN — 2nd letter to Americans. GOLD — $4,867/oz. Gas $4.076 (ticking down). Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Ceasefire expires April 22 — 4 days. Next talks Monday Islamabad.
verificationNote: Compiled from PBS, NBC News, Trading Economics, Iran LiveUAMap, AAA.

text: HORMUZ CHAOS: Iran reverses Strait of Hormuz opening within hours. Ghalibaf scrapped reopening deal citing US 'untrustworthiness.' IRGC gunboats fired on tanker 20 miles NE of Oman — UKMTO confirmed, no injuries. Two more merchant vessels hit. Armed Forces accuses US of 'piracy and maritime robbery.' Oil surging on renewed uncertainty.
verificationNote: NBC News, CBS News, Irish Times, The National confirmed reversal and gunboat incident.

text: TRUMP SIGNALS: At Arizona Turning Point rally Trump announced Hormuz 'fully open' (already reversed by Iran). On Air Force One said 'some pretty good news 20 minutes ago' but declined to elaborate. Warned ceasefire may end without deal by Wednesday. Blockade 'remains in full force.' Next talks expected Monday Islamabad.
verificationNote: Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post confirmed.

text: DAY 50 AM ROUNDUP: Iran pushes back on Trump deal claims. Hormuz shut down again — Iran cites US 'piracy.' Enriched uranium sticking point — US says 'going very well,' Iran denies agreeing to handover. CENTCOM: 21 vessels turned back. Lebanon ceasefire violations continue. US sanctions 7 Iraqi militia commanders. Brent $97.68 (+22.3%).
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, NBC News, Tribune India, US Treasury confirmed.

text: NUCLEAR STALEMATE: Iran FM rejects Trump's enriched uranium handover claim — 'will not be transferred anywhere.' US demanded 20-year moratorium, Iran countered with 5 years. ~450kg at 60% purity at issue. $20B cash-for-uranium framework still under discussion. Next talks expected Monday Islamabad. Ceasefire expires April 22.
verificationNote: Tribune India, Bloomberg confirmed.

text: Day 50 AM roundup: HORMUZ — Iran reverses opening, IRGC gunboats fire on tanker (no injuries). Ghalibaf: 'America's untrustworthiness.' TRUMP — 'good news' at Arizona rally, blockade 'in full force.' NUCLEAR — Iran rejects uranium handover. SANCTIONS — 7 Iraqi militia commanders designated.
